context: Anthony's personal notes on which family expenses he absorbs and which ones get split. Not a company policy. Used to keep his own reimbursement-tracking honest when logging invoices against Dale, Tammy, Janelle, or Ray.

validation_rules.notes: Groceries, handyman visits, and pharmacy runs for Mama Jean stay off the split ledger. Anthony covers these quietly and never asks her to repay.
validation_rules.requirement: Log the outflow against the Xero family-support account only, not against a family-member customer.
validation_rules.rule: Mama Jean support is fully absorbed and stays out of the reimbursement column.

validation_rules.notes: When Anthony drives up to Tuscaloosa with Dale, they split fuel roughly in half. Anthony fronts the fill-up and logs Dale's half as an open reimbursement.
validation_rules.requirement: Receipt from the fill-up plus a note of the destination trip.

validation_rules.notes: If Janelle asks Anthony to cover something in a pinch (textbook, unexpected car repair), he fronts it and lets her repay on the next visit without pressure.
validation_rules.requirement: Personal note in the QuickBooks invoice's PrivateNote field so Anthony remembers the context.
validation_rules.rule: Janelle's incidentals get fronted; repayment is soft-tracked, not chased.

validation_rules.notes: When Ray Campos and Anthony share a truck-stop room to save money on a shared corridor, they split the room 50/50.
validation_rules.requirement: Room receipt plus the shared corridor date.
validation_rules.rule: Ray's half of any shared-room truck-stop night is a real reimbursement, logged as an open invoice.

validation_rules.notes: If Tammy asks Anthony to grab Sunday-dinner groceries on his way through Birmingham, he fronts them and she settles at the dinner table.
validation_rules.requirement: Grocery receipt tucked into the QuickBooks invoice.
validation_rules.rule: Tammy's Sunday-dinner grocery runs are fronted and settled the same week.

validation_rules.notes: Anything Anthony buys for himself on the road (coffee, showers, meals) is his own cost even when other drivers are present.
validation_rules.rule: Personal road spending is Anthony's alone and never enters the split column.
